chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No.D-4001 of 2022 (Muhammad Aslam v. Chief Executive Officer PSM and others) ______________________________________________________________ Date Order With Signature Of Judge ______________________________________________________________ Before:- Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Date of hearing and order:- 10.02.2026 Syed Nadeem-ul-Haq, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Feroz Ahmed, advocate for the respondent. Ms. Wajiha Mehdi, Assistant Attorney General. ------------- O R D E R Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J.- Petitioner Muhammad Aslam has filed this petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. The petitioner was initially appointed as Machine Operator (HSW- III) in 1983, later transferred to Pakistan Steel Mills, and retired after an unblemished service record. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner admits that the admitted dues have been received; however, payment of a further amount of Rs.527,318/- is disputed. 3. Pursuant to the directions of this Court, respondent No.2 deposited an amount of Rs.7,348,721/- with the Nazir of this Court on 05.06.2024. It is further stated that the petitioner has already received Rs.6,821,403/- as undisputed dues, while an amount of Rs.527,318/- is presently lying with the Nazir. The respondents contend that rent of the official accommodation was not properly adjusted. The petitioner was retrenched on 02.07.2021, vacated the accommodation on 16.12.2024, and rent is claimed as under:  Normal rent up to 05.06.2024: Rs.53,690/-  Standard rent from 06.06.2024 to 16.12.2024: Rs.127,205/-  Total rent: Rs.180,805/- 4.

The respondents contend that rent of the official accommodation was not properly adjusted. The petitioner was retrenched on 02.07.2021, vacated the accommodation on 16.12.2024, and rent is claimed as under:  Normal rent up to 05.06.2024: Rs.53,690/-  Standard rent from 06.06.2024 to 16.12.2024: Rs.127,205/-  Total rent: Rs.180,805/- 4. It is further submitted that an amount of Rs.227,296/- relating to pay scale revision of 2010 has been withheld, as the same has not been released by the Government of Pakistan. 5. The Nazir of this Court is directed to reconcile the rent and dues by hearing the parties and to release the balance amount, if any, to the petitioner within 15 days. 6. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Shahzad Soomro
